title: IntegerSliderCommandInput Object
description: Provides a command input to get the value of a slider from the user, the value type is integer.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getText
methods.description: Gets the texts of the slider if text has been defined.

methods.name: setText
methods.description: Sets the text of the slider. Both the left and the right text should be set.

properties.name: commandInputs
properties.description: Gets the CommandInputs class of the parent, which can be a Command, GroupCommandInput or TabCommandInput.

properties.name: expressionOne
properties.description: Uses an expression to set the value in the first input field. This can contain equations and is evaluated using the specified unit type.

properties.name: expressionTwo
properties.description: Uses an expression to set the value in the second input field. This can contain equations and is evaluated using the specified unit type.   This property is only available when the hasTwoSliders property returns true.

properties.name: hasTwoSliders
properties.description: Gets if the command input has two sliders.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets the unique identifier for this input in the command's CommandInputs.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.description: Gets or sets if this input is currently enabled or disabled for user interaction.   Currently, the isEnabled property does not disable SelectionCommandInput objects but instead has the same effect as the SelectionCommandInput.hasFocus property.

properties.name: isFullWidth
properties.description: Gets or sets if this input fills the entire width of the dialog. If true, the name is ignored and the input control will fill the entire width of the command dialog. The default value for this property in a new command input if false, or not to fill the width. This property does not apply to GroupCommandInputs or TabCommandInputs.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets or sets if this input will be visible to the user.   Setting a SelectionCommandInput to be invisible will clear any selections it currently has.

properties.name: maximumValue
properties.description: Gets and sets maximum value of the slider. This will fail if there is a value list because the minimum and maximum values are defined by the value list.

properties.name: minimumValue
properties.description: Gets and sets minimum value of the slider. This will fail if there is a value list because the minimum and maximum values are defined by the value list.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets the user visible name of this input.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentCommand
properties.description: Gets the parent Command.

properties.name: parentCommandInput
properties.description: Gets the parent CommandInput if this commandInput is the child of a TabCommandInput or GroupCommandInput. Returns null if there is no parent.

properties.name: spinStep
properties.description: Gets and sets the spin step. This defines the amount the slider moves when the user clicks the spin button beside the value. The spin step should be more than zero.

properties.name: toolClipFilename
properties.description: Gets or sets the full filename of the image file (PNG) used for the tool clip. The tooltip is always shown but as the user hovers over the control it will progressively display the tool clip and description text.

properties.name: tooltip
properties.description: Gets or sets the base tooltip string. This is always shown for commands. If the tooltip description and/or tool clip are also specified then the tooltip will progressively display more information as the user hovers the mouse over the control.

properties.name: tooltipDescription
properties.description: Gets or sets additional text to display progressively along with the tooltip. The text for the description can contain some basic HTML formatting tags to format the tags. For example the br tag can be used to create multiple paragraphs. The tooltip is always shown but as the user hovers over the control it will progressively display the tool clip and description text.

properties.name: unitType
properties.description: Gets and sets the unit type that is used when evaluating the user's input.

properties.name: valueList
properties.description: Gets the value list of the slider. The list is empty if this command input was not added by value list.

properties.name: valueOne
properties.description: Gets or sets the value associated with the first slider.

properties.name: valueTwo
properties.description: If the hasTwoSliders property is true, this returns the value associated with the second slider.

related_samples.name: Command Inputs API Sample
related_samples.description:
Creates a command dialog that demonstrates all of the available command inputs.

To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the default code.  You also need to unpack this zip file which contains a resource folder into the same folder where the source code file (.py or .cpp) is.

classType.title: IntegerSliderCommandInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.IntegerSliderCommandInput.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::IntegerSliderCommandInput::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: IntegerSliderCommandInput.deleteMe Method

syntax.python: returnValue = integerSliderCommandInput_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = integerSliderCommandInput_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

getText.title: IntegerSliderCommandInput.getText Method
getText.description: Gets the texts of the slider if text has been defined.

syntax.python: returnValue = integerSliderCommandInput_var.getText(isLeft)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = integerSliderCommandInput_var->getText(isLeft);

getText.return_description: Returns the left or right text of the slider.

parameters.name: isLeft
parameters.description: Indicates to get the left or right text.

setText.title: IntegerSliderCommandInput.setText Method
setText.description: Sets the text of the slider. Both the left and the right text should be set.

syntax.python: returnValue = integerSliderCommandInput_var.setText(left, right)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = integerSliderCommandInput_var->setText(left, right);

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Indicates the text on the left side of the slider.

parameters.name: right
parameters.description: Indicates the text on the right side of the slider.
